commit.message:
[feature][QSR] Add HW exception handling (#40654)

### Ticket
N/A

### Problem description
HW provides exceptions for illegal instruction etc. that weren't handle

### What's changed
Added an ISR to handle hardware exception that will set a WATCHER assert
if enabled. The assert will include the cause, the offending value and
the value of PC when it happened. ISR will also handle DFB related soft
interrupts - now also have an option to disable the handler if implicit
sync isn't needed.
NOTE: Added tests that force HW exceptions, for some reason only 2 types
were causing actual exceptions, couldn't get misaligned store/load or
load access fault to trigger.

tests](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/workflows/sanity-tests.yaml/badge.svg?branch=ayaacob/handle_errors)](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/workflows/sanity-tests.yaml?query=branch:ayaacob/handle_errors)

Fix Immich photos: proxy thumbnails through API too

Immich requires x-api-key header for all requests including
thumbnails. CSS background-image can't send custom headers, so
route image URLs through /api/immich-proxy as well. Proxy now
handles both JSON and binary responses with proper Content-Type.
Images cached for 24h to reduce proxy load.

commit.message:
test: e2e for vote session, elimination, and state restore on refresh

Components - add data-testid attributes:
- VotingPanel: voting-panel-active/select, vote-player-btn-{name},
  vote-message-input, close-vote-btn, vote-outcome-banner,
  confirm-eliminate-btn, start-new-vote-btn, yes-count, no-count,
  accused-player-name, vote-count
- VoteOverlay: vote-overlay, overlay-accused-name, vote-yes-btn,
  vote-no-btn, overlay-outcome, dismiss-overlay-btn
- GameStarted: moderator-phase-label, moderator-round-number

Backend: gameToJSON now includes phase + round_number (fixes restore)

test-helpers.ts - new helpers:
- openVoteSessionFor, waitForVoteOverlay, castBallotOnOverlay
- closeVoteFromPanel, startNewVoteFromPanel
- waitForModeratorPhaseLabel, waitForModeratorRound
- setupGameInProgress (full game setup in one call)

e2e/vote-session.spec.ts - 16 scenarios:
- Moderator opens vote → players see overlay (with/without message)
- Player votes yes/no → live count updates on moderator side
- Player changes vote before close (yes → no)
- Multiple players vote → correct yes/no counts
- Close vote → majority banner + confirm-eliminate / spared result
- Player dismisses overlay after close
- Start new vote for different player after close
- New overlay replaces dismissed overlay for new session
- Majority yes + confirm eliminate → player removed from alive list,
  panel resets automatically (no Start New Vote needed)
- Moderator refresh restores day/night phase + round number
- Moderator refresh restores open vote session in active panel
- Player refresh restores day phase badge
- Player refresh restores night phase badge
- Player refresh while vote is open → overlay reappears
- roleSeen persists across refresh

commit.message:
Bidirectional cloud sync: push local settings if cloud is empty

On app startup, initCloudSync now checks both directions:
- Cloud has data → merge into local (existing behavior)
- Cloud empty + local has API keys → push local up to cloud
- Widget data (localStorage) also syncs both ways

This fixes the issue where settings entered while the API was broken
stayed trapped in one domain's IndexedDB and never reached the cloud.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Protect cloud save: merge with existing data, never overwrite with empty

saveAllToCloud now pulls existing cloud settings first and merges —
empty local fields won't clobber populated cloud values. Prevents
accidental key loss when saving from a fresh browser session.

Make settings cloud sync reliable: await sync, log errors

Previously all cloud syncs were fire-and-forget (.catch(() => {})) so
failures were completely silent. Now saveSettings() awaits the sync and
logs any errors. cloudPut() also logs the response body on failure for
easier debugging.

Fix API: read collection from PUT body when not in query params

params. Updated the API handler to parse the body first for PUT/POST to
extract collection, then reuse the parsed body to avoid double-parsing.

commit.message:
fix(node): increase main task stack from 16 KB to 24 KB (#516)

* fix(node): increase main task stack from 16 KB to 24 KB

Stack overflow (Guru Meditation: Stack protection fault) during
PEER_REQUEST after BLE provisioning. The HMAC-SHA256 computation +
CBOR encoding + ESP-NOW frame construction exceeds 16 KB.

Increase CONFIG_ESP_MAIN_TASK_STACK_SIZE from 16384 to 24576.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review findings: clarify ND-0918 comment, derive stack size in CI

- sdkconfig.defaults: reword comment to clarify ND-0918 requires >= 16 KB
  and we configure 24 KB for headroom after observed overflow
- esp32.yml: derive expected stack size from sdkconfig.defaults instead of
  hardcoding, preventing future drift

* docs: add ops cockpit design and architecture plan

* fix: query live_capacity/status by aircraft SN not RC SN

live_capacity and live_status are stored in the DB under the aircraft
(sub-device) SN because they arrive on the aircraft state topic.
The REST poll was using selectedSn (RC/gateway SN) causing null returns
that overwrote valid WS-pushed capacity, reverting UI to FALLBACK.

- Use bundle.sub_device.sn (aircraftSn) for getDjiLiveCapacity/Status
- Add setCapacity(prev => cap ?? prev) guard against DB write race

* fix: prevent OSD empty live_capacity from overwriting good state capacity

The RC Plus 2 OSD topic fires at 4 Hz. When the OSD payload contains a
live_capacity key (common before streaming starts), the backend was
broadcasting it even when device_list was empty or absent. This caused
the frontend to briefly show the correct '4 STREAM(S)' from the state
topic and then immediately flip to 'FALLBACK' as the first OSD message
wiped the capacity.

Backend (mqtt/router.py _handle_osd):
- Only broadcast/persist live_capacity from OSD if device_list is
  non-empty. The state topic remains the authoritative source; OSD is
  only used as a supplemental update when it carries actual stream data.

Frontend (useLiveStream.ts):
- pushCapacity: guard against WS empty-cap overwriting a good capacity
  (don't downgrade if existing capacity has streams and new push is empty)
- loadCapacityAndStatus: strengthen REST-poll guard from 'not null' to
  'has non-empty device_list', preventing an empty REST response from
  also triggering the downgrade when WS push has raced ahead

* fix: live_status stale REST race, capacity DB protection, single lens selector

Fixes 'quality green/no text', 'NO STREAM' after start, page-refresh
capacity fallback, and duplicate lens dropdowns.

Backend (mqtt/router.py _handle_state):
- Only write live_capacity to DB when device_list is non-empty; still
  broadcast all values so the frontend stays real-time. This ensures
  that page refreshes always read the last-good capability profile and
  prevents transient empty state updates (e.g. RC negotiation phase)
  from wiping the stored streams.

Frontend (useLiveStream.ts):
- Add liveStatus downgrade guard: same race exists for live_status as
  for live_capacity. The REST poll returns live_status:[] before the
  async DB write completes. Don't overwrite a WS-pushed active stream
  status (status=1) with the empty REST response.
- Remove deviceBundles from loadCapacityAndStatus deps (unused).

Frontend (StreamControls.tsx, Feeds.tsx):
- Replace the raw video-ID stream selector + separate Lens dropdown
  with a single Lens selector. When capacity lists streams, options
  are derived from those streams (Normal/Wide/Zoom/IR mapped from
  video_id suffix). When no capacity (fallback mode), static Lens
  options still appear. Removes the 'two lens dropdowns' confusion.

* fix: guard OSD live_status broadcast + WS keepalive ping

Two root causes for 'NO STREAM after start' and 'intermittent telemetry':

Backend (mqtt/router.py _handle_osd):
- Same issue as capacity: OSD fires at 4 Hz and may carry live_status
  with status=0 or [] during the gap between stream-start command and
  the authoritative state-topic response. Broadcasting those would
  overwrite the active live_status and cause 'NO STREAM' flicker.
  Now only broadcasts/persists OSD-sourced live_status when at least
  one stream shows status=1. The state topic remains authoritative for
  both start AND stop transitions.

Backend (routers/ws.py telemetry_ws):
- WS handler blocked on queue.get() indefinitely with no keepalive.
  When OSD stops (aircraft stationary, no movement) the queue is empty
  and the Caddy/nginx proxy closes the idle connection after ~60s.
  Frontend reconnects after 3s but during that window all real-time
  pushes (telemetry, live_capacity, live_status) are lost.
  Now uses asyncio.wait_for(queue.get(), timeout=20) and sends a
  JSON ping frame on timeout to keep the proxy connection alive.

* fix: stop OSD from driving stream state — WS signal-only architecture

Root cause of all blinking/flickering: OSD fires at 4 Hz and was broadcasting
live_capacity and live_status over WS. The frontend then called triggerRefresh
(REST refetch) on every broadcast — 4-8 REST calls/sec with concurrent in-flight
fetches racing each other, causing constant state churn and card blinking.

Changes:
- router.py _handle_osd: remove live_capacity and live_status broadcasts entirely.
  State topic (_handle_state) is the exclusive source for both — it fires only on
  real state changes (pushMode=1), not 4 Hz. OSD now only drives positional
  telemetry.
- router.py _handle_state: already awaits DB write before broadcasting (from
  previous commit), retaining the guarantee that REST refetch sees fresh data.
- useLiveStream.ts: replace pushCapacity/pushLiveStatus with triggerRefresh
  (signal-only, no data carried). Add in-flight guard to prevent concurrent REST
  fetches from racing if WS signals arrive in quick succession.
- useTelemetryWS.ts: simplified to onStreamStateChanged callback (no data), fires
  only on live_capacity/live_status WS messages (now only from state topic).
- Feeds.tsx: memoize selectableBundles/bundlesForUi so the selectedSn-sync effect
  does not fire on every OSD-driven render (4 Hz).

* refactor: stateless Feeds page — load once, refresh on demand, OSD-only WS

- Rewrite Feeds.tsx as fully stateless: no polling, no WS stream callbacks
- Load devices+config+status on mount and on explicit Refresh
- Start/Stop via REST POST with 1.5s/1s settle delay then re-fetch status
- useTelemetryWS simplified to OSD-only (remove onStreamStateChanged)
- Remove dead files: useLiveStream.ts, StreamControls.tsx

* fix: unwrap _ok() envelope in getDjiLiveStatus/Config/updateConfig

GET /dji/live/config, PUT /dji/live/config, GET /dji/live/status all
return {code, message, data: T} but callers expected T directly.

- liveConfig.url_type was undefined → url_type dropped from JSON.stringify
  → backend 422 'url_type is required' on stream start
- liveConfig.url was undefined → URL showed blank in Feeds and Settings
- Settings save appeared to not work (URL display was unwrapping wrong field)

Fix: extract .data from the _ok() envelope in these three functions.

* feat: live lens selection on Feeds page

- Fetch live_capacity for selected device (on mount + device change)
- Derive switchable_video_types from active stream's camera/video entry

  is correctly populated (fixes lens toggle buttons never appearing)

* fix: lens change uses target video_id not current stream id

Per DJI Cloud API spec, live_lens_change requires the TARGET stream's
video_id (e.g. SN/39-0-7/wide-0), not the currently-active video_id.
Sending the current id with a different video_type caused the drone to
silently ignore the command (no ACK → 'failed code 0').

Derive targetVideoId by replacing the lens portion of video_index:
  activeStream.video_id = 'SN/39-0-7/normal-0' + type 'wide'
  → targetVideoId       = 'SN/39-0-7/wide-0'

* fix: RC Plus lens change — use 'ir' type, revert DRC fallback

- RC Plus video_type for thermal/IR is 'ir' not 'thermal' (RC Plus 2 uses 'thermal')
- Add 'ir' to VIDEO_LENS_TYPES validator; was wrongly rejecting RC Plus thermal
- Frontend LENS_TYPES: replace 'thermal' with 'ir' to match RC Plus spec
  and construct correct video_id 'SN/cam/ir-0' (not 'thermal-0')
- Revert /dji/live/lens DRC fallback: was shortening the 8s services
  timeout to 3s and issuing RC Plus 2 drc/down commands RC Plus ignores
- DRC router helpers retained for future RC Plus 2 support (inert for now)
- Improved lens change error message: shows 'timeout', 'drone error code N',
  or 'not acknowledged' instead of generic 'code 0'
- Update test: test_validate_lens_change_ir_valid asserts 'ir' is accepted

* fix: tighten video_type to enum per DJI spec

* fix: cross-SN services_reply correlation for live_lens_change

live_lens_change is a camera-level operation — the RC Plus forwards it to
the aircraft, and the reply arrives on thing/product/{AIRCRAFT_SN}/services_reply
instead of the gateway SN topic we published on.

The existing correlator matched (sn, method, tid) and had a fallback for
missing-method replies on the same SN, but no fallback for a different SN.

Add a tid-only cross-SN fallback as a third tier: if direct and same-SN
lookups both fail, scan pending replies matching only the tid field. Each
tid is unique per-request (timestamp + uuid fragment) so false matches are
not possible.

Also adds a debug log when the cross-SN path fires, to confirm the hypothesis
once deployed against hardware.

* fix: normalise ir→thermal in live_lens_change, simplify services_reply correlation

Per DJI Cloud API spec, live_lens_change video_type enum is 'normal', 'thermal',

the command must use 'thermal' for infrared. Add _IR_ALIAS normalisation in
validate_method_payload.

_handle_service_reply: remove incorrect cross-SN tier-3 fallback (spec says
reply always arrives on the same gateway_sn). Add debug log to show raw reply
result/tid so we can see what the device sends when lens change fires.

Adds scripts/host_setup/pi_ubuntu_hardened.sh — a security-hardened
variant of the existing pi_ubuntu_setup.sh for field deployments.

Hardening measures applied:
- Unattended-upgrades for automatic security patches
- Docker Engine with restricted socket permissions (660)
- Dedicated deploy user (password-locked, SSH key only)
- SSH daemon: no root login, no password auth, configurable port,
  AllowUsers restricted to deploy user, ForwardingNo
- fail2ban: SSH jail with 3-failure / 24h ban policy
- sysctl network hardening: SYN cookies, anti-spoofing rp_filter,
  ICMP broadcast ignore, source-route disable, redirect disable
- sysctl kernel hardening: dmesg_restrict, ptrace_scope=1,
  kptr_restrict=2, protected hard/symlinks
- UFW firewall: deny-by-default, mission net CIDR for 80/443/8883,
  SSH restricted to management CIDR
- Disabled: avahi-daemon, cups, bluetooth, ModemManager
- Audit daemon with file-change rules for /etc/passwd, sudoers,
  /etc/shadow, sshd_config, root command logging
- Chrony NTP (required for DJI certificate chain validation)
- Scoped NOPASSWD sudo for Docker and deploy systemctl ops

Script is idempotent and supports --dry-run, --skip-ufw,
--skip-ssh-harden, and runtime configuration flags.

* fix: migration 015 UUID type mismatch crashing API on fresh install

Migration 015 added preferred_drone_profile_id as String(36) with a FK
to drone_profiles.id which is a native UUID column (created by migration
010). PostgreSQL rejects incompatible FK types, causing the API container
to crash on every cold start, leaving the DB with no tables.

Fix: use postgresql.UUID(as_uuid=True) in migration 015 to match the
referenced column type. Also update the PilotProfile model annotation
from Mapped[str | None] to Mapped[uuid.UUID | None] to match the DB.

Closes: fresh-install migration failure on Pi.

* fix: bind-mount data/maps into tileserver + api, remove HSTS, fix captive portal 204

- Tileserver was using named volume 'maps_data' which was empty; mbtiles
  files live in ./data/maps on the host. Switch both tileserver (ro) and
  api (rw) to a bind mount so Martin auto-detects the files on startup.
  Remove the now-unused maps_data named volume declaration.

- Remove Strict-Transport-Security from Caddyfile. The entire site is
  served over HTTP (explicit http:// prefix); HSTS on HTTP is ignored by
  spec-compliant browsers and should never be set on non-HTTPS responses.

- Fix captive portal catch-all: respond 204 to Android/Chrome connectivity
  probe paths (/generate_204, /gen_204, /connectivity-check) so the OS
  stops showing a 'Sign in to Wi-Fi' banner once the operator is on the
  network. All other unknown hosts still redirect to the AP IP.

* fix: install_vector_pack.sh copies active.mbtiles instead of symlinking

Martin (tile server) resolves symlinks to their real path and uses the
actual filename stem as the source name. A symlink named active.mbtiles
pointing to illinois_20260304.mbtiles results in Martin registering a
source named 'illinois_20260304', not 'active'.

The gateway Caddyfile routes /tiles/* -> /active/{z}/{x}/{y} on Martin,
so the file must literally be named active.mbtiles.

Replace 'ln -sf PACK active.mbtiles' with 'cp PACK active.mbtiles' so
Martin always sees a source named 'active'. The versioned pack is still
kept as a separate copy for reference/rollback.

  to avoid string drift between map instances
- Move NavigationControl to top-right in useMapInit (was bottom-right,
  colliding with AttributionControl in the same corner)
- Fill in empty CSS rules for .maplibregl-ctrl-attrib dark theme

* fix: pi_setup.sh hardening — dnsmasq unmask, dns-redirect shebang, /etc/hosts, teardown script (#203)

* fix: unmask dnsmasq, add shebang to dns-redirect, fix /etc/hosts on hostname change

* chore: add pi_teardown.sh for clean reinstall testing

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments on pi setup/teardown scripts

pi_setup.sh:
- Fix /etc/hosts guard to target 127.0.1.1 line specifically, not broad
  hostname string match; tighten sed pattern to [[:space:]] boundary (#6, #7)
- Grant docker+dialout groups to REPO_OWNER when it differs from DEPLOY_USER
  so docker compose can access /var/run/docker.sock (#1)
- Fix dns-redirect fallback interface wlan1 -> wlan0 to match setup default (#5)

pi_teardown.sh:
- Read WIFI_IFACE before config files are removed; delete only the specific
  iptables rules pi_setup adds instead of flushing entire chains (#3)
- Preserve DEPLOY_USER's Planetiler OSM cache before userdel -r removes
  their home directory; restore to /root/.cache/shadowhound-maps (#4)
- Replace fragile sed hostname reset with awk for robust 127.0.1.1 update
  that handles aliases and missing entries correctly (#2)

* feat: Meshtastic mesh network integration (#204)

* feat: Meshtastic mesh network integration

- Backend: MeshNode model (node_id, lat/lon, snr, hops, battery, included_in_mission)
- Backend: meshtastic serial service (15s poll, async, auto-retry on disconnect)
- Backend: REST router GET /mesh/status + /mesh/nodes, PATCH /mesh/nodes/{id}/include
- Backend: WS broadcast of mesh_node updates via shared telemetry queue
- Config: MESHTASTIC_DEVICE env var (empty string = disabled/no-op)
- Frontend: Mesh page with node roster, include/exclude toggle, auto-refresh
- Frontend: OpsView mesh layer (cyan circles), WS live updates, overlay toggle
- Frontend: Mesh nav link visible to ADMIN and PLANNER roles

* feat: expose meshtastic_device in Settings UI

- Add meshtastic_device column to system_settings model/table
- Expose in GET/PUT /admin/settings; restart service on change
- On startup, prefer DB setting over env var (DB wins)
- Add Mesh section to Settings page with device path input

* fix: update import path for require_role in mesh router

* fix: remove duplicate activeDrones declaration in OpsView

* fix: mesh node overlay + race fixes follow-up (#204) (#205)

* fix: mesh nodes race condition + gateway offline timing (#204)

* fix: seed mesh nodes directly in map load callback, eliminate race condition

* fix: use flock to prevent duplicate serial gateway in multi-worker uvicorn

* feat: add mesh node overlay to MapView incident map (#204)

- Add mesh-nodes source + mesh-circles/mesh-labels layers to useMapInit
- Hover popup on mesh circles (name, ID, hops, SNR, last heard)
- Add showMeshNodes toggle to OverlayControl
- Add Mesh Node legend entry to MapLegend
- Load included mesh nodes in MapView.loadMapData
- Sync mesh source via useEffect with mapLoaded state dep
- Fix race: onMapLoaded callback sets React state so mesh sync
  effect re-runs after map is ready, not just after data loads

* fix: address PR #205 review comments

- useMapInit: coerce p.hops_away to Number before comparison so direct
  links display correctly when MapLibre returns stringified properties
- meshtastic_service: persist lock_fd in module-level _lock_fd global so
  the file descriptor is not immediately GC'd and the flock released
- meshtastic_service: narrow except OSError to BlockingIOError/EAGAIN so
  genuine lock errors (bad path, full disk) are not silently swallowed
- OpsView: remove duplicate api.listMeshNodes() fetch inside map.on('load')
  — setMapLoaded(true) already triggers the existing mesh-sync effect which
  reuses the REST seed fetched on mount

* chore: GPS socat relay setup in pi_setup.sh (#206)

* chore: add gpsd socat relay setup to pi_setup.sh and update gps test script

- pi_setup.sh step 10: install gpsd, gpsd-clients, socat
- Create gps-relay.service (socat -u unidirectional relay: /dev/ttyUSB0 → /tmp/gpsd-pty)
  Prevents gpsd probe bytes from reaching GPS chip and silencing it
- Configure /etc/default/gpsd to use /tmp/gpsd-pty relay PTY
- Add gpsd.service.d override to depend on gps-relay.service
- Add AppArmor local override to allow gpsd access to /dev/pts/*
- Append chrony SHM refclock (idempotent) for GPS-disciplined time
- quick_gps_test_ubuntu.sh: detect running relay stack and attach to
  existing gpsd socket instead of starting a conflicting instance

* fix: address Copilot review comments on GPS relay setup

- PTY moved from /tmp/gpsd-pty (mode=666) to /run/gpsd-relay/pty (mode=660)
  with RuntimeDirectory=gpsd-relay so path is under /run, not world-writable /tmp
- gps-relay.service: BindsTo= instead of Requires=; WantedBy=dev-ttyUSB0.device
  instead of multi-user.target (auto-start on device plug, stop on unplug)
- AppArmor override: append-only idempotent rules instead of cat > overwrite
- pi_setup.sh: conditionally start relay immediately if /dev/ttyUSB0 present;
  informational message otherwise (avoids failed-state on deviceless setup)
- quick_gps_test_ubuntu.sh: also check gpsd.socket for socket-activation detection
- quick_gps_test_ubuntu.sh: scope gpsd kill to GPS_DEVICE via pkill -f instead
  of killall gpsd (avoids terminating unrelated gpsd instances)
- Update docs/OFFLINE_TIME_SETUP.md and docs/GPS_TESTING.md to match new paths

* fix: mesh gateway shows DISABLED inconsistently with multiple workers (#207)

* fix: set _device before lock check so all workers report correct device path

With --workers 2, only the worker that acquires the flock owns the serial
gateway and was the only one to set _device. The other worker returned
early with _device still empty, so its get_status() returned device: ''
causing the UI to show 'DISABLED' instead of 'OFFLINE'. Round-robin
between workers produced the inconsistency (sometimes DISABLED, sometimes
OFFLINE depending which worker answered the request).

Move _device = device_path above the lock acquisition so every worker
knows the configured device. Only the lock-owning worker connects, but
all workers correctly report the device path in /api/mesh/status.

* fix: map MESHTASTIC_DEVICE into api container in pi compose override

The api container previously had devices: [] (empty), so the Meshtastic
serial node (/dev/ttyACM0) was not accessible inside the container.
The service would log connection failed (No such file or directory).

Use MESHTASTIC_DEVICE env var (from .env) to map the device through.
Falls back to /dev/null when unset so the compose file stays valid
for deployments without a Meshtastic node.

* fix: use fetch+reset in update.sh to handle divergent branches (#208)

* fix: run single uvicorn worker on Pi to eliminate mesh status race

With --workers 2 (the default Dockerfile CMD), the meshtastic serial
gateway is owned by one worker while the other reports connected=false,
causing the UI to alternate between ONLINE and OFFLINE on round-robin.

Override to --workers 1 in the Pi compose file. A single worker is
sufficient for SAR field deployments and avoids the shared-state problem
entirely.

* fix: use fetch+reset instead of pull in update.sh to handle divergent branches

git pull fails when the deployment host has local commits (e.g. from
cherry-picks applied during hotfixes). Use git fetch + git reset --hard
origin/<branch> instead, which always syncs to the exact remote state
regardless of local commit divergence.

* docs: add SETUP_GUIDE.md and link from README (#209)

End-to-end first-time deployment guide covering:
- Step 1: Ubuntu install
- Step 2: pi_setup.sh (GPS relay, Docker, systemd service)
- Step 3: .env configuration (including MESHTASTIC_DEVICE)
- Step 4: docker compose up with Pi overlay
- Step 5: admin account creation
- Step 6: map tiles verification
- Step 7: DJI Pilot 2 connection
- Step 8: GPS/time sync verification
- Step 9: web UI verification

Also adds MESHTASTIC_DEVICE to the env variable table and
corrects update.sh description (fetch+reset, not git pull).

* fix: update.sh detects Pi/Jetson overlay from systemd service (#211)

On Pi, update.sh was running bare 'docker compose' without the
docker-compose.pi.yml overlay, stripping device mappings and the
--workers 1 override on every update. The Meshtastic gateway then
failed with 'No such file or directory: /dev/ttyACM0'.

Fix: read the installed shadowhound.service to detect which compose
overlay was configured by pi_setup.sh, and use those same -f flags
for all docker compose calls (down, up --build, ps).

* fix: address PR #212 review comments

- Add auth (get_current_user) to all /mesh GET endpoints (router-level)
- Fix asyncio.get_event_loop() deprecated -> get_running_loop()
- Fix _device assignment order in start() -- set before early return
- Fix Number(null)==0 bug in hops_away popup (useMapInit.ts, OpsView.tsx)
- Fix void now eslint anti-pattern in Mesh.tsx useEffect
- Update UI copy: device config source -> Settings page reference
- Widen meshtastic_device column String(64) -> String(256)
- Add Alembic migration 020: mesh_nodes table + meshtastic_device column

* fix: correct Feeds tests and NO STREAM state

- Mock getDjiLiveCapacity in Feeds.test.tsx (was missing, causing
  statusError to appear and hiding IDLE/LIVE cards)
- Add default mockGetLiveCapacity.mockResolvedValue in beforeEach
- Fix Force Stop button test to use correct label 'Stop Stream'
- Show 'NO STREAM' in stream status card when live_status is null
  (vs IDLE when status is present but no active streams)
- Apply formatter-driven whitespace normalisation to 020_mesh_integration.py
  and meshtastic_service.py

* fix: resolve TS6133 unused variable 'now' in Mesh.tsx

Pass the 'now' timestamp state into formatLastHeard() so TypeScript
recognises it as consumed. This also makes the relative timestamps
(e.g. '2m ago') correctly re-render every 30s when the state ticks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[ci] E: Store versioned releases

Replace the ephemeral "latest" release with versioned releases tagged
v{MAJOR}.{MINOR}.{PATCH}. Each CI run now creates a unique, permanent
release instead of deleting and recreating a single "latest" release.

Key changes:
- Tag releases as v{version} instead of "latest".
- Add idempotency guard: skip gracefully if release already exists.
- Handle concurrent-creation race (422 on createRelease).
- Simplify tag creation (no retry loop needed for unique tags).
- Remove release-deletion step; keep stale draft cleanup.
- Maintain "latest" git tag as a moving pointer for compatibility.
- Rename publish-latest job to publish-release in ci.yml.
